Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Kamitakata 3628 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Takatahime-no-Mikoto, Tsukawakehiko-no-Mikoto, Sazumorihiko-no-Mikoto, Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto, Kibitakehiko-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Traditionally {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514|1=Bitchū Province}} {{ill|lt=Ninomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Tsutsumi Gosha Daimyōjin, Tsutsumi-sama |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Tsutsumi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tsutsumi Shrine''' (つつみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and is traditionally regarded as the ninomiya of {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514|1=Bitchū Province}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} is "Dancing Crane." === Enshrined deities === '''Main deity''' * Takatahime-no-Mikoto - wife of Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto '''Merged deities''' * {{ill|lt=Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|Q65318|WD=Q65318|1=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto}} - said to have pacified Kibi as one of the {{ill|lt=Shidō shōgun|Q9076804|WD=Q9076804|1=Shidō Shōgun}} * Kibitakehiko-no-Mikoto - son of Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto * Sazumorihiko-no-Mikoto - local powerful clan chief who ruled this area as {{ill|lt=agatanushi|Q10746487|WD=Q10746487|1=Agatanushi}}, father of Takatahime-no-Mikoto * Tsukawakehiko-no-Mikoto - retainer of Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto === History === The '{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}' and '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' include legends of Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto pacifying Kibi, and this shrine enshrines figures related to that story. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' lists "Tsutsumi Shrine" in Kaya District of Bitchū Province, ranking it as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It is said there were once five shrine buildings and it was called "Tsutsumi Gosha Daimyōjin"[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=19062 Tsutsumi Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Shrine Association).. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, in {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 21 ({{ill|lt=1644|Q6920|WD=Q6920|1=1644}}), Kinoshita Toshiate, lord of {{ill|lt=Ashimori Domain|Q10863289|WD=Q10863289|lt=Ashimori Domain|1=Ashimori Domain}}, donated 2 koku of shrine lands"Place Names of Okayama Prefecture," Tsutsumi Shrine section.. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5, under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and in Meiji 14 it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Sessha and massha === * Itsukushima-gū, Inari Shrine, and others === Main festivals === * New Year's Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}}) * Summer purification festival ({{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774|1=August 15}}) * Autumn festival (second Sunday of October) === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National designation) === * Hōtō pagoda *: Made of granite, 4.15 m high. Estimated late Kamakura period production. Designated in 1959. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Kamitakata 3628 ** Approx. 3 km northwest of {{ill|lt=Okayama Airport|Q1322391|WD=Q1322391|1=Okayama Airport}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * "Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture" ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Okayama City, Tsutsumi Shrine section === External links === * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/18517/ Tsutsumi Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsutsumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsutsumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:岡山市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Kamitakata 3628 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Takatahime-no-Mikoto, Tsukawakehiko-no-Mikoto, Sazumorihiko-no-Mikoto, Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto, Kibitakehiko-no-Mikoto |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Traditionally {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514|1=Bitchū Province}} {{ill|lt=Ninomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Tsutsumi Gosha Daimyōjin, Tsutsumi-sama |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Tsutsumi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tsutsumi Shrine''' (つつみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and is traditionally regarded as the ninomiya of {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|WD=Q1047514|1=Bitchū Province}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} is "Dancing Crane." === Enshrined deities === '''Main deity''' * Takatahime-no-Mikoto - wife of Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto '''Merged deities''' * {{ill|lt=Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|Q65318|WD=Q65318|1=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto}} - said to have pacified Kibi as one of the {{ill|lt=Shidō shōgun|Q9076804|WD=Q9076804|1=Shidō Shōgun}} * Kibitakehiko-no-Mikoto - son of Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto * Sazumorihiko-no-Mikoto - local powerful clan chief who ruled this area as {{ill|lt=agatanushi|Q10746487|WD=Q10746487|1=Agatanushi}}, father of Takatahime-no-Mikoto * Tsukawakehiko-no-Mikoto - retainer of Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto === History === The '{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}' and '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}' include legends of Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto pacifying Kibi, and this shrine enshrines figures related to that story. In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' lists "Tsutsumi Shrine" in Kaya District of Bitchū Province, ranking it as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. It is said there were once five shrine buildings and it was called "Tsutsumi Gosha Daimyōjin"[http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=19062 Tsutsumi Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Shrine Association).. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, in {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 21 ({{ill|lt=1644|Q6920|WD=Q6920|1=1644}}), Kinoshita Toshiate, lord of {{ill|lt=Ashimori Domain|Q10863289|WD=Q10863289|lt=Ashimori Domain|1=Ashimori Domain}}, donated 2 koku of shrine lands"Place Names of Okayama Prefecture," Tsutsumi Shrine section.. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5, under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and in Meiji 14 it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Sessha and massha === * Itsukushima-gū, Inari Shrine, and others === Main festivals === * New Year's Day Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}}) * Summer purification festival ({{ill|lt=August 15|Q2774|WD=Q2774|1=August 15}}) * Autumn festival (second Sunday of October) === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (National designation) === * Hōtō pagoda *: Made of granite, 4.15 m high. Estimated late Kamakura period production. Designated in 1959. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|WD=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okayama|Q200078|WD=Q200078|1=Okayama}}{{ill|lt=Kita-ku (Okayama)|Q855478|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}Kamitakata 3628 ** Approx. 3 km northwest of {{ill|lt=Okayama Airport|Q1322391|WD=Q1322391|1=Okayama Airport}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * "Japan Historical Place Name Compendium: Place Names of Okayama Prefecture" ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Okayama City, Tsutsumi Shrine section === External links === * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/18517/ Tsutsumi Shrine] (Okayama Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsutsumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsutsumi Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:岡山市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kita-ku, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Okayama]]
